stories.headline: Trump Says End to Iran War in Sight, Spurring Market Rally
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump indicated he may be preparing to wind down the war with Iran, boosting market optimism and restoring some stability to global energy prices.

stories.headline: US military says blockade of Iran ports ‘completely’ halts economic trade
stories.summary: US Central Command say more than 10,000 armed forces members, as well as warships and planes, are involved in blockade.

stories.headline: Iran war live: Trump says war ‘close to over’ as Hormuz blockade continues
stories.summary: US president says second round of talks could be held in Pakistan as naval blockade of Iranian ports continues.

stories.headline: Trump accuses Italy’s Meloni of not having 'courage' to back Iran war
stories.summary: Donald Trump attacks European ally Meloni for refusing to back the war on Iran and for defending Pope Leo XIV, sparking a rare show of unity among Italian parties.

stories.headline: Trump hints Iran talks could resume this week as US port blockade continues
stories.summary: The United Nations' secretary general said it was "highly probable" that the negotiations would restart.

stories.headline: Iran faces halt to oil production within weeks if US blockade succeeds
stories.summary: Tehran has a maximum of 16 days of storage before it will need to curtail its output

stories.headline: Rubio Hosts Israel and Lebanon for Rare Meeting Shadowed by U.S.-Iran War
stories.summary: The gathering ended with encouraging words, even as Israel continued to refuse to halt its military campaign against Iran-backed Hezbollah in Lebanon.

stories.headline: Trump turns on Meloni, saying she lacks ‘courage’ over US-Israel war on Iran
stories.summary: The US president says he is 'shocked at her', delivering a blunt public rebuke to one of his closest European allies.

stories.headline: War in Iran Is Already Reshaping East Asia’s Energy Future
stories.summary: The war in Iran has caused the price of all kinds of commodities to surge, and that has a negative economic impact almost everywhere. But the squeeze is really being felt hard in East Asia, which is the ultimate destination for a lot of oil and gas that come out of the Gulf. And though the Strait of Hormuz may eventually re-open, and the acute pain may pass, this episode may already be reshaping the future. On this episode of the podcast we speak

stories.headline: Norway’s Crude Exports Hit Record Value as Oil Price Soared
stories.summary: Norway’s crude exports surged to a record by value last month due to the outbreak of the war in the Middle East, helping to raise the country’s trade surplus to the highest level in more than three years.

stories.headline: Private healthcare in the UK: helping or harming the NHS?
stories.summary: Critics worry the country is heading towards a two-tier system. Dentistry is a cautionary tale

stories.headline: ‘Bifs’ replace ‘Piigs’ as Europe’s bond market whipping boys

stories.headline: Swalwell’s Exit Injects ‘Chaos’ Into California Governor’s Race
stories.summary: Democratic candidates see sudden voter interest in the sleepy contest as a campaign opportunity. All are scrambling for support from former backers of Eric Swalwell.

stories.headline: Gallego Dismissed Rumors of a ‘Flirty’ Swalwell, Highlighting a Culture of Silence
stories.summary: The admission by Senator Ruben Gallego that he had heard, but disbelieved, rumors about Eric Swalwell and women showed the attitude on Capitol Hill toward men accused of behaving badly.

stories.summary: Pakistan welcomed around 2,300 Sikh pilgrims from India for Baisakhi celebrations at Gurdwara Panja Sahib in Hasan Abdal, about 45 kilometres from Islamabad.

stories.headline: Oil prices fall as renewed hopes for peace talks feed a stock market rally
stories.summary: Shares mostly rose in early Wednesday trading, echoing the rally on Wall Street that came as oil prices eased on hopes that the United States and Iran may resume talks.

stories.headline: Peru election dispute deepens amid slow ballot count
stories.summary: Peru saw hundreds of protesters gather outside the Electoral Tribunal in Lima on Tuesday 15 April 2026, demanding a rerun of the presidential election after voting disruptions delayed the process.

stories.headline: Crowds in Bratislava reject Fico plan to scrap overseas voting
stories.summary: Thousands rallied in Bratislava and other cities on Tuesday, 14 April 2026, to oppose Prime Minister Robert Fico’s plan to scrap mail voting for Slovaks abroad, warning it could undermine democratic participation.

stories.headline: The mix of chaotic politics and a resilient economy can’t last

stories.headline: The China Show 4/15/2026 (Video)
Pimco Buys All $400 Million of Bonds Sold by Blue Owl BDC
stories.summary: Pacific Investment Management Co. bought all $400 million of bonds issued Monday by a Blue Owl Capital Inc. private credit fund, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

stories.headline: Pimco Buys All $400 Mln of Bonds Sold by Blue Owl BDC
stories.summary: Pacific Investment Management Co. bought all $400 million of bonds issued Monday by a Blue Owl Capital Inc. private credit fund, according to people with knowledge of the matter. The deal is a vote of confidence for a private credit industry that’s been battered by record redemption requests and deepening concerns over valuations, liquidity and transparency. Bloomberg’s Sinead Cruise reports.

stories.headline: Chinese Nickel Tycoon Taps Top Global Traders for Aluminum Push
stories.summary: A Chinese tycoon who helped transform Indonesia’s metals industry over the past decade is in talks with commodity trading giants Mercuria Energy Group, Glencore Plc and Trafigura Group to secure investments in his new, $3 billion aluminum smelter.

stories.headline: Russia Shuts Down $13 Billion Tax Fraud, Investigators Say
stories.summary: Russian investigators said they broke up an organized crime group in Moscow that was responsible for more than 1 trillion rubles ($13.3 billion) in tax losses to the state budget, which is already strained by a widening deficit.

stories.headline: Pakistan Secures $3 Billion in Saudi Loans to Lift Reserves
stories.summary: Pakistan said it secured $3 billion in financial support from Saudi Arabia, helping to bolster its foreign exchange reserves and offset a loan repayment the country will make to the United Arab Emirates.

stories.headline: Candidate calls for new election in Peru, claiming fraud without evidence
stories.summary: Peru’s presidential candidate Rafael Lopez Aliaga is urging officials to declare Sunday’s election 'null and void’.

stories.summary: Palestinians in Gaza recorded victims of an Israeli strike on the Shati refugee camp being rushed to a medical facility.

stories.headline: Watch: What are Harry and Meghan doing in Australia?

stories.headline: Justice Dept. Moves to Vacate Jan. 6 Convictions for Far-Right Extremists
stories.summary: Defending the convictions would likely have required administration officials to assert that far-right groups were acting on behalf of President Trump on Jan. 6, 2021.

stories.headline: Resignations of Eric Swalwell and Tony Gonzales Set Up Special Election Fights in Texas and California
stories.summary: Gov. Gavin Newsom of California called a special election to replace Mr. Swalwell, while Gov. Greg Abbott of Texas had yet to announce one for Mr. Gonzales’s successor.

stories.headline: A New Accuser Says Eric Swalwell Sexually Assaulted Her
stories.summary: The woman said Mr. Swalwell, who resigned from Congress on Tuesday afternoon, raped her in a West Hollywood hotel room in 2018. She said she believed she was drugged.

stories.headline: Trump Administration Accuses Biden DOJ of Unfairly Prosecuting Anti-Abortion Activists
stories.summary: The report is part of the president’s effort to claim anti-conservative and anti-Christian biases in federal law enforcement, even as he pushes to wield the legal system against his political enemies.

stories.headline: DOJ moves to undo Jan. 6 rioters’ convictions for seditious conspiracy
stories.summary: President Donald Trump last year commuted the prison sentences of 12 members of the Proud Boys and Oath Keepers.

stories.headline: What Swalwell’s exit means for Democrats in California’s governor race
stories.summary: Billionaire Tom Steyer and ex-congresswoman Katie Porter stand to benefit the most in a Democratic field with no clear front-runner, several strategists said.

stories.headline: JD Vance defends backing 'great guy' Orbán's campaign after landslide defeat
stories.summary: Hungary's leader was a "great guy" who did a "very good job", the US vice-president said, but he was sure he could work with Péter Magyar.

stories.headline: Spain approves plan to give around 500,000 undocumented migrants legal status
stories.summary: Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez said migrants helped "build the rich, open and diverse Spain that we are and to which we aspire".

stories.headline: Harry and Meghan mix charity and business on Australia visit
stories.summary: There are questions over whether local taxpayers are picking up any of the bill for policing the visit.

stories.summary: US stocks are holding firm near an all-time high after a rally powered by optimism around a potential peace deal in the Middle East, with traders now eyeing confirmation of a new round of talks.

stories.headline: Oil Swings as Traders Decode Talk Signals With Hormuz Closed
stories.summary: Oil fluctuated as traders took stock of moves to arrange a second round of peace talks between the US and Iran, as well as the near-total, double blockade of flows of crude through the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: Spain’s Bangladeshi community cheers migrant amnesty programme
stories.summary: Crowds from the Bangladeshi community in Barcelona celebrated the approval of a mass amnesty programme.

stories.headline: Israel steps up strikes on Lebanon and Hezbollah attacks continue as talks loom
stories.summary: More than 160 people have been killed since Friday in Lebanon, where Israel is pressing on with its campaign against Hezbollah despite the US-Iran ceasefire.

stories.headline: As Iran war drags on, midterm forecasts for Republicans get even worse
stories.summary: The GOP was already bracing for a tough election, but with the ceasefire in doubt, gas prices are likely to stay high, and voters’ economic outlook is dismal.

stories.headline: Hungary's next PM would pick up if Putin calls and tell him to stop Ukraine war
stories.summary: Russia has offered "pragmatic" relations, but Péter Magyar says he will not call Vladimir Putin himself.

stories.headline: Peru election drags into second day after ballot delivery fiasco
stories.summary: Technical and logistical problems mean Peruvians choosing a new president and members of Congress continue to vote on Monday.

stories.headline: UK could adopt EU single market rules under new legislation
stories.summary: The move has raised questions over parliamentary scrutiny of future rules to deliver planned EU deals.

stories.headline: Orbán era swept away by Péter Magyar's Hungary election landslide
stories.summary: Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule is over, defeated by a 45-year-old ex-party insider who convinced a majority of Hungarians to oust him.

stories.headline: 'A truly historic moment': Hungarian opposition wins election landslide
stories.summary: Rajini Vaidyanathan broadcasts from outside Hungary's parliament as crowds hear about the prime minister's concession.

stories.headline: Philippines accuses Chinese fishermen of dumping cyanide in South China Sea
stories.summary: Beijing rejects the allegation as a "farce", while Manila claims the poison was used to kill fish and deprive troops stationed in the area of food.

stories.headline: Ukraine and Russia accuse each other of hundreds of ceasefire violations
stories.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky says his nation's forces would respond "symmetrically" to Russian attacks.

stories.headline: Argentina passes bill loosening protection of its glaciers
stories.summary: The new bill will make it easier for provincial governments to allow mining projects in glacier regions.

stories.headline: Israel backs US-Iran ceasefire but Netanyahu's war goals remain unfulfilled
stories.summary: The Israeli prime minister's response to the ceasefire has been muted, in contrast to the triumphal statements from the US and Iran.
